articles.title: A Tide-Fueled Trove of Biodiversity in Guinea-Bissau
articles.summary: The expansive mudflats, sandy beaches, and mangrove forests of the Bijagós archipelago support an array of migratory shorebirds and large numbers of sea turtles.

articles.title: No-Go For Starship Today, Next Try Maybe Next Week

articles.title: SpaceX aborts Starship Flight 13 launch attempt
articles.summary:
SpaceX aborted the launch of its latest Starship test flight July 16 when some of the vehicle’s engines failed to ignite.
The post SpaceX aborts Starship Flight 13 launch attempt appeared first on SpaceNews.

articles.title: NASA Welcomes Serbia as Newest Artemis Accords Signatory
articles.summary: The Republic of Serbia signed the Artemis Accords Thursday during a ceremony hosted by NASA at the agency’s Headquarters in Washington, becoming the 69th nation to join a large community of like-minded nations committed to the peaceful, transparent, and responsible exploration of space. “Serbia’s connection to NASA reaches back to the Apollo program, when the […]

articles.title: Russia Agrees to ISS Operations, Seat-Swaps, Through 2030
articles.summary: During meetings this week with NASA Administrator Jared Isaacman, Roscosmos Director General Dmitry Bakanov agreed that Russia will support operations of the International Space Station through 2030. Until now, Russia […]

articles.title: SpaceX launches 21 satellites for military data network
articles.summary:
With the launch of 21 York Space Systems satellites, the Space Development Agency has now placed half of its planned Tranche 1 Transport Layer constellation in orbit

articles.title: Nature study identifies a new way to spot nuclear weapons in orbit
articles.summary:

articles.title: NASA Study Finds Near-Earth Asteroid Is Actually Comet
articles.summary: New research led by scientists at NASA’s Jet Propulsion Laboratory in Southern California has revealed the identity of a puzzling near-Earth object by precisely tracking its motion through space and using powerful observatories that image faint celestial objects. This object has a dual personality: Past images hadn’t revealed obvious cometlike activity, suggesting it might be […]
